blocks.statement: I can solve a horizontal-launch projectile problem: find fall time from height, then find horizontal range.

blocks.whatWeKnow: 2026-XJ has both horizontal and vertical velocity (with respect to Earth).
blocks.connection: Cliff problems are the small-scale version. The recipe (find t first, then x) is the same recipe orbit problems use.

blocks.markdown:
## The two-step recipe

Every horizontal-launch problem is the same two steps, in the same order:

1. **Find time from the VERTICAL motion:** h = ½·g·t² → t = √(2h/g). The height alone decides how long it's in the air.
2. **Find range from the HORIZONTAL motion:** x = vₓ·t. Plug in the t you just found.

Never the other way around. The horizontal motion can't tell you the time — vₓ never changes, so it has no clock in it.

blocks.title: Strategy every time
blocks.markdown: **FIND TIME FIRST** from the vertical motion. Then plug t into x = vₓ·t.

blocks.title: Test it in the simulation
blocks.markdown: Launch a few projectiles below. Change the height and watch the fall time change; change vₓ and watch the range change while the fall time stays put. That asymmetry IS the independence from yesterday.

blocks.prompt: A ball is fired horizontally from a 5 m cliff at vₓ = 10 m/s. (a) How long until it hits the ground? (b) How far from the cliff base?
blocks.given: h = 5 m · vₓ = 10 m/s · g = 9.81 m/s²
blocks.answer: t ≈ 1.0 s, lands ≈ 10 m from the cliff.

blocks.instruction: Pick your own numbers for cliff height h and launch speed vₓ. Draw the cliff, the launch arrow, and trace the parabola lightly. Label the range x where it lands.
prompts: Label h on the cliff and vₓ on the launch arrow.
The path curves down more and more steeply — a parabola.
Label the range x along the ground.

blocks.prompt: A ball is fired horizontally from a 45 m cliff at vₓ = 15 m/s. (a) Find fall time. (b) Find horizontal range.
blocks.equationHint: Step 1: h = ½·g·t² gives the time. Step 2: x = vₓ·t gives the range.

blocks.prompt: From a 20 m cliff at vₓ = 8 m/s, where does the ball land? Show both steps.
blocks.frame: Step 1 (time from height): t ≈ ___ s. Step 2 (range): x = 8 × ___ ≈ ___ m.

blocks.title: Read & practice
blocks.markdown: Open the reader and read **5.5–5.6** (projectiles launched horizontally and projectiles launched at an angle). Answer the questions beside the reading as you go.
